2603.17070.title: Large Reasoning Models Struggle to Transfer Parametric Knowledge Across Scripts
2603.17070.paper_summary: In this work, we analyze shortcomings in cross-lingual knowledge transfer in large, modern reasoning LLMs. We demonstrate that the perceived gap in knowledge transfer is primarily a script barrier. First, we conduct an observational data analysis on the performance of thinking models on two datasets with local knowledge from around the world, ECLeKTic and MultiLoKo. Our regression analysis shows that script match - not language or family - is the primary predictor of knowledge transfer failure once model capability and question difficulty are accounted for. We further this finding by providing the LLMs with the key entities of the questions in their source language and find that this disproportionately improves cross-script questions. We then posit that these LLMs could be reasoning better at test-time. To evaluate this, we develop a synthetic generation pipeline to design SFT samples to encourage the model to better reason about transliteration ambiguities when trying to fetch parametric knowledge at inference-time. We show that teaching two models to reason better reduces the cross-script transfer gap. As a result, we conclude that there is potential to improve cross-lingual parametric knowledge transfer during post-training.
2603.17070.paper_summary_zh: 在這項工作中，我們分析了大型現代推理 LLM 中跨語言知識轉移的缺陷。<lb/>我們證明了知識轉移的感知差距主要是一個文字系統障礙。<lb/>首先，我們對來自世界各地的兩個數據集 ECLeKTic 和 MultiLoKo 中思考模型的表現進行了觀察性數據分析。<lb/>我們的回歸分析顯示，文字系統匹配——而非語言或語系——是知識轉移失敗的主要預測因子，當考慮到模型能力和問題難度後更是如此。<lb/>我們通過向 LLM 提供問題的關鍵實體及其源語言進一步驗證了這一發現，並發現這對跨文字系統問題的改善效果不成比例。<lb/>然後我們假設這些 LLM 在測試時可能能更好地推理。<lb/>為了評估這一點，我們開發了一個合成生成管道，設計 SFT 樣本以鼓勵模型在推理時更好地推理音譯歧義。<lb/>我們顯示，教導兩個模型更好地推理可以減少跨文字系統的轉移差距。<lb/>因此，我們得出結論，後訓練期間有潛力改善跨語言參數知識轉移。
2603.17070.author: Lucas Bandarkar et.al.
2603.17070.authors: Lucas Bandarkar, Alan Ansell, Trevor Cohn
